title: lora_l2.c + lichen_l2.c: peer_table cleared by disable but not by deinit
description:
Cross-file interaction inconsistency: peer_table is cleared in lichen_l2_enable(false) at lines 1293-1297, but NOT in lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() or during iface re-init after failure.

lichen_l2.c:1293-1297 during disable:
  for (i=0; i < MAX_NEIGHBORS; i++) {
    secure_zero(peer_table[i].pubkey, ...);
    peer_table[i].active = false;
  }

But lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() at line 1691 only reinits rx_mutex, does not clear peer_table.

SCENARIO:
1. Peers added to peer_table
2. RX thread abort occurs
3. lichen_lora_l2_deinit() calls lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort()
4. lichen_lora_l2_init() called again
5. lichen_l2_iface_init() runs, checks lichen_iface != NULL (line 1596), sets iface_init_failed
6. Init fails but peer_table still has old entries with pubkeys

ACTUAL: Line 1596 check prevents re-init after first init, so this scenario requires reboot anyway.

BETTER SCENARIO:
1. lichen_l2_enable(true) after abort recovery
2. needs_reinit() was true, deinit() called (line 1192)
3. deinit() calls reinit_after_abort() which does NOT clear peer_table
4. But deinit() returns, disable path continues at line 1219 with HAVE_LICHEN_LINK cleanup
5. Actually line 1267-1297 DOES clear peer_table after reinit_after_abort()

WITHDRAW: Following the code paths, peer_table IS cleared by the disable path that calls deinit(). The cleanup happens AFTER reinit_after_abort().
close_reason: not-a-bug: peer_table cleared by disable path after reinit_after_abort
